info.title: Pocket Guide POI Metadata API
info.description: API for managing POI metadata including coordinates, hours, and travel distances

get.description:
Initiate Google OAuth login for BACKSTAGE admin panel

Args:
    redirect_uri: Backstage callback URL
    code_challenge: PKCE code challenge (SHA256 of verifier)

Returns:
    Authorization URL and state parameter

Initiate Google OAuth login for CLIENT APP

Args:
    redirect_uri: Client app callback URL (web or mobile)
    code_challenge: PKCE code challenge (SHA256 of verifier)

Returns:
    Authorization URL and state parameter

DEPRECATED: Use /auth/backstage/google/login or /auth/client/google/login instead

Initiate Google OAuth login (legacy endpoint with auto-detection)

Args:
    redirect_uri: Frontend callback URL
    code_challenge: PKCE code challenge (SHA256 of verifier)

Returns:
    Authorization URL and state parameter

Handle Google OAuth callback for BACKSTAGE admin panel

Args:
    code: Authorization code from Google
    state: CSRF protection state parameter
    code_verifier: PKCE code verifier

Returns:
    JWT access token and refresh token for backstage user

Raises:
    HTTPException: If OAuth fails or user not authorized

Handle Google OAuth callback for BACKSTAGE WEB UI

This endpoint redirects to the backstage UI with code and state,
allowing the browser to complete the PKCE flow with its stored code_verifier.

Args:
    code: Authorization code from Google
    state: CSRF protection state parameter

Returns:
    Redirect to backstage UI with code and state in URL hash

Handle Google OAuth callback for CLIENT APP

Args:
    code: Authorization code from Google
    state: CSRF protection state parameter
    code_verifier: PKCE code verifier

Returns:
    JWT access token and refresh token for client app user

Raises:
    HTTPException: If OAuth fails or user not authorized

DEPRECATED: Use /auth/backstage/google/callback or /auth/client/google/callback instead

Handle Google OAuth callback (legacy endpoint with auto-detection)

Args:
    code: Authorization code from Google
    state: CSRF protection state parameter
    code_verifier: PKCE code verifier

Returns:
    JWT access token and refresh token

Raises:
    HTTPException: If OAuth fails or user not authorized

post.description:
Refresh access token using refresh token

Args:
    request: Refresh token request

Returns:
    New access token (same refresh token)

Raises:
    HTTPException: If refresh token is invalid or expired

Logout user and invalidate session

Args:
    request: Refresh token to invalidate
    current_user: Current user from JWT token

Returns:
    Success message

get.description:
Get current user information

Args:
    current_user: Current user from JWT token

Returns:
    User information

Debug endpoint to check OAuth configuration and see exact URL generated

Test with: /auth/debug/oauth-config?redirect_uri=http://localhost:3000/auth/callback

Returns:
    - What client type would be detected
    - What client_id would be used
    - EXACT Google OAuth URL that would be generated
    - All parameters being sent to Google

post.description:
Verify Google ID token from native SDK and return app tokens

This endpoint is for mobile apps using native Google Sign-In SDK.
The SDK provides an ID token which we verify with Google, then
return our own access_token and refresh_token.

Args:
    request: ID token from Google Sign-In SDK

Returns:
    JWT access token and refresh token for client app user

Raises:
    HTTPException: If token verification fails or user not allowed

Verify Apple identity token from native Sign In with Apple SDK.

The mobile app calls this after a successful Sign In with Apple flow.
We verify the identity_token cryptographically using Apple's public keys,
then issue our own access_token + refresh_token.

Args:
    request.identity_token: Signed JWT from Apple (always required)
    request.authorization_code: One-time code from Apple (optional, used
        to exchange with Apple's token endpoint on first sign-in)

Returns:
    JWT access token and refresh token for the client app user

Raises:
    401: If identity_token is invalid, expired, or claims don't match
    503: If Apple's public keys cannot be fetched

delete.description:
Permanently delete the authenticated user's account.

- Revokes all active sessions immediately
- Anonymises personal data in user_data/ files and tour metadata
- Renames user_data/{email}/ directory to user_data/deleted_{hash}/
- Records deletion in config/deleted_accounts.json (audit log only)
- The user can re-register as a fresh account after deletion

Returns 200 on success.

get.description:
List all combo tickets for a city.

Args:
    city: City name (e.g., "rome", "paris")

Returns:
    List of combo tickets with metadata

post.description:
Create a new combo ticket for a city.

Args:
    city: City name
    ticket: Combo ticket data

Returns:
    Success response with created ticket ID

get.description:
Get a specific combo ticket by ID.

Args:
    city: City name
    ticket_id: Combo ticket ID

Returns:
    Combo ticket details

put.description:
Update an existing combo ticket.

Args:
    city: City name
    ticket_id: Combo ticket ID to update
    update: Updated fields

Returns:
    Success response

delete.description:
Delete a combo ticket and remove references from POIs.

Args:
    city: City name
    ticket_id: Combo ticket ID to delete

Returns:
    Success response

get.description:
Validate combo ticket data for consistency.

Checks:
- All members in combo tickets are valid POIs
- All POIs referencing combo tickets have valid IDs
- Bi-directional consistency

Args:

Returns:

Get all combo tickets that include a specific POI.

Args:

    poi_name: POI name

Returns:

post.description:
Generate an AI-powered walking tour itinerary.

This endpoint:
1. Uses AI to select optimal POIs based on preferences
2. Optimizes the visiting sequence using selected algorithm
3. Creates a day-by-day itinerary
4. Saves the tour if requested

Args:
    request: Tour generation parameters
    current_user: Optional authenticated user info (auto-injected)

Returns:
    Generated tour data with itinerary and metadata

get.description:
Get the status of a tour generation request.

This is useful for async generation in the future.

Args:
    tour_id: Tour ID or generation task ID

Returns:
    Generation status

post.description:
Create a new manually-built tour.

Enriches POIs with metadata, computes walking distances/times, saves
sectioned transcripts, and persists the tour in the standard structure.

put.description:
Update an existing manually-built tour (or any tour by ID).

Saves a new version for the given language while preserving previous
versions and other-language files untouched.

get.description:
Load an existing tour for editing in the manual creator.

Returns itinerary + sectioned transcripts merged together so the
frontend can pre-populate all fields.

schema.title: Response Get Manual Tour Tour Manual  Tour Id  Get

post.description:
Generate a personalized tour for authenticated client app users.

This endpoint:
1. Uses AI to select optimal POIs based on user preferences
2. Optimizes the visiting sequence using the selected algorithm
3. Creates a day-by-day itinerary
4. Saves the tour as PRIVATE (only visible to creator)

**Authentication Required**: Client app users only

**Tour Visibility**: Tours created via this endpoint are PRIVATE by default.
Only the creator and backstage admins can view these tours.

**Parameters**:
- `city`: City name (required)
- `days`: Number of days (1-14, required)
- `interests`: List of interests (optional)
- `must_see`: POIs that must be included (optional)
- `pace`: Trip pace - relaxed, normal, or packed (default: normal)
- `walking`: Walking tolerance - low, moderate, or high (default: moderate)
- `language`: Tour language ISO code (default: en)
- `mode`: Optimization mode - simple or ilp (default: simple)
- `start_location`: Starting point (optional)
- `end_location`: Ending point (optional)
- `start_date`: Trip start date for checking hours (optional)
- `provider`: AI provider (default: anthropic)

**Example Request**:
```json
{
  "city": "rome",
  "days": 3,
  "interests": ["history", "architecture"],
  "pace": "normal",
  "walking": "moderate",
  "language": "en"
}
```

**Returns**: Tour details with itinerary, optimization scores, and tour ID

get.description:
List all tours created by the current user.

**Authentication Required**: Client app users only

**Returns**: List of tours with metadata, sorted by creation date (newest first)

post.description:
Save POI completion status for authenticated user.

Marks a POI as completed or not completed for the current user.
Each user has their own independent progress tracking.

get.description:
Get all POI completion statuses for authenticated user.

Returns completion status for all POIs in the tour, including:
- POI ID and name
- Day number
- Completion status
- Completion timestamp (if completed)
- Overall completion percentage

post.description:
Save GPS trail points for authenticated user (batch upload).

Accepts up to 100 GPS points per request. Client should batch points
and upload every 1 minute OR when 20 points collected, whichever comes first.

get.description:
Get all GPS trail points for authenticated user.

Returns complete GPS trail for the tour, including all recorded points
with latitude, longitude, and timestamp.

post.description:
Save audio section progress for a POI.

Mobile calls this on:
- All sections complete (immediate POST)
- App goes to background (flush pending)
- App is killed (best-effort flush)

Progress is stored per-user so it survives app reinstalls and syncs across devices.

get.description:
Fetch all audio section progress for the tour.

Mobile calls this once on tour load to seed the local resume cache.
No polling — single fetch per session.

parameters.name: language

schema.description: Tour language (unused, kept for API symmetry)
schema.title: Language

parameters.description: Tour language (unused, kept for API symmetry)

post.description:
Trigger GPS trail inference for POI completion (post-launch feature).

Backend analyses the stored GPS trail and marks POIs as visited when the
user was within 80m for ≥ 60 consecutive seconds. Only fills gaps —
never overwrites an explicit completion from the mobile client.

Currently a stub: accepts the request and returns success.
Full inference will be implemented as a background job in a later release.

Upload image for a POI (backstage admin only)

- Maximum 5 images per POI
- File size limit: 5MB
- Formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP
- Images are compressed automatically

get.description: Get list of images for a POI (public access)
Serve POI image file (public access)

post.description:
Upload image for a tour (backstage admin only)

- 1 cover image + max 10 gallery images
- File size limit: 5MB
- Formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP
- Images are compressed automatically

get.description: Get images for a tour (public access)
Serve tour image file (public access)

post.description:
Upload batched GPS coordinates from background or foreground tracking.

This endpoint supports the client app's background location tracking feature.
It accepts extended GPS data including altitude, heading, and speed.

Background tracking uploads:
- Collected every 30 seconds
- Batched and uploaded every 1 minute
- Includes upload_type: "background"

Foreground tracking uploads:
- Collected every 5 seconds
- Can be uploaded more frequently
- Includes upload_type: "foreground"

Args:
    tour_id: Tour identifier
    request: Batch of GPS coordinates with metadata
    user_info: Authenticated user information

Returns:
    Status of the upload including number of coordinates received

Called when the user taps **Start Tour** and enters the map screen.

Any existing `in_progress` session for the same tour is silently closed
as `abandoned` before the new session is created (the user will not see this).

Returns a `session_id` that the frontend must store and pass to all
subsequent progress/end calls.

patch.summary: Update audio progress for one section of a POI
patch.description:
Update playback position for one audio section within a POI.

**When to call:**
- User pauses audio
- App goes to background
- Section completes (set `completed=true`)

**poi_visited:** Send `true` the first time the user arrives at a POI
on the map — this is separate from audio playback and marks the physical visit.

This endpoint is idempotent — sending the same position twice is safe.

post.description:
Close a tour session and record the final stats.

**When to call:**
- User taps **Back** / exits the map screen → `status="ended"`
- User taps **Finish Tour** (all POIs completed) → `status="completed"`

`total_duration_seconds` is calculated server-side from `started_at` to now.

get.description:
Return the authenticated user's tour history, newest first.

Used to render the **History** screen. Each item maps to one card in the list:
- `tour_title` → card title
- `city_slug` → subtitle
- `started_at` → date shown (e.g. "May 2026")
- `duration_days` → "1 day" label
- `status` → show "In Progress" badge if `in_progress`

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status: in_progress, ended, completed, abandoned
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by status: in_progress, ended, completed, abandoned

schema.title: Response Get History Client History Get

get.summary: Get full detail for one tour session
get.description:
Return full session detail including per-POI, per-section audio progress.

The `resume` field is populated for `in_progress` and `ended` sessions —
it points to the last half-played section and will be used by the
**Resume Tour** feature to restore playback position.

Used for the landing page carousel and sitemap generation.

schema.description: Tour language code e.g. en, zh-tw
schema.title: Language

get.description: API root endpoint
Health check endpoint

List all active user sessions (admin only).

Returns information about logged-in users including email, role, scopes, and last access time.

List all cities with POI data.

Returns a list of cities with their POI counts from content directory.

Update city-level config (emoji, etc.). Admin only.

Persists to config/city_config.json so changes survive server restarts.
Currently supports: emoji.

get.description:
List all POIs for a specific city.

Returns summary information for each POI including metadata status.
Loads from content directory (content/{city}/) instead of poi_research.

schema.title: Response List City Pois Cities  City  Pois Get

get.description:
Get detailed metadata for a specific POI.

Returns all metadata including coordinates, hours, and visit information.
Tries to load from content directory first, then falls back to poi_research.

put.description:
Update metadata for a specific POI.

Allows manual editing of coordinates, visit info, and other metadata fields.
Saves to content directory if POI exists there, otherwise to poi_research.

post.description:
Re-collect metadata from Google Maps API for a specific POI.

Useful for refreshing outdated information.

get.description:
Get the distance matrix for all POIs in a city.

Returns travel times and distances for walking, transit, and driving.

post.description:
Recalculate the distance matrix for a city.

Uses Google Maps Distance Matrix API to calculate fresh travel times.

Collect metadata for all POIs in a city.

This endpoint triggers full metadata collection including:
- POI coordinates and details from Google Maps
- Distance matrix calculation

get.description:
Verify metadata completeness for all POIs in a city.

Returns a report showing which POIs have complete metadata
and which fields are missing.

Get transcript and summary for a POI in a specific language.

If tour_id is provided, will try to use the tour's linked transcript.
Falls back to standard POI transcript if tour link doesn't exist.

Args:
    city: City name (e.g., "Athens")
    poi_id: POI identifier in kebab-case (e.g., "acropolis-parthenon")
    language: ISO 639-1 language code (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'es') - defaults to 'en'
    tour_id: Optional tour ID to load tour-specific transcript link

Returns:
    TranscriptData with transcript text, summary points, availability flags, and language info

put.description:
Update transcript for a POI in a specific language (creates backup before saving).

Args:
    city: City name (e.g., "Athens")
    poi_id: POI identifier in kebab-case (e.g., "acropolis-parthenon")
    update: TranscriptUpdate with new transcript text and optional language code

Returns:
    SuccessResponse with backup filename

get.description:
Get sectioned transcript with knowledge points and titles.

If tour_id is provided, will try to use the tour's linked custom sectioned transcript.
Falls back to standard sectioned transcript if tour link doesn't exist.

Args:
    city: City name
    poi_id: POI identifier
    language: Language code (default: en)
    tour_id: Optional tour ID for tour-specific transcripts

Returns:
    Sectioned transcript data with sections array

Serve audio file for POI section or full audio.

Args:
    city: City name
    poi_id: POI identifier
    filename: Audio filename (e.g., audio_section_1_en.mp3 or audio_en.mp3)

Returns:
    MP3 audio file

Get research data for a POI (both structured and raw YAML).

Args:
    city: City name (e.g., "Athens")
    poi_id: POI identifier in kebab-case (e.g., "acropolis-parthenon")

Returns:
    ResearchData with structured fields and raw YAML content

Return the list of categories derived from public tours, sorted by tour count descending.

Optionally scope to a city with ?city=rome.

The category list is dynamic — it reflects what actually exists in the tour library.
TODO (future): extend into a recommendation system that personalises category order
using user signals (past tours, stated interests, location, time of day).

List all saved tours, grouped by city.

Returns list of tour summaries with key metadata.

Get complete tour details including itinerary, backup POIs, and selection transparency.

Args:
    tour_id: Tour identifier (e.g., "rome-tour-20260129-111100-aa7baf")
    language: ISO 639-1 language code (e.g., 'en', 'zh-tw') - defaults to 'en'

Returns:
    Complete tour details with all metadata

Get reviews for a tour (public, no auth required).

Currently returns an empty list — review submission system is not yet implemented.
See TODO-LIST.md item #8 for the full implementation plan.

post.description:
Soft-delete a tour (backstage admin only).

Sets active=false in metadata. Tour is hidden from client API
but data is preserved and can be restored via /activate.

Restore a deactivated tour (backstage admin only).

Sets active=true in metadata. Tour becomes visible to client API again.

get.description:
Get transcript links for a tour.

Returns which transcripts are linked to this specific tour.
This allows the tour to reference specific transcript versions.

Args:
    tour_id: Tour identifier (e.g., "rome-tour-20260205-121250-11edf4")
    language: Language code (e.g., 'en', 'zh-tw')

Returns:
    TranscriptLinks with all POI transcript mappings for this tour

post.description:
Replace a POI in a tour with a backup POI.

Supports two modes:
- simple: Replace POI and keep current order/timing
- reoptimize: Replace POI and re-run itinerary optimizer (NOT YET IMPLEMENTED)

Args:
    tour_id: Tour identifier
    request: POI replacement request with mode, POI names, language, and day

Returns:
    POI replacement response with success status and new version info

Replace multiple POIs in a tour with backup POIs in a single operation.

DEFENSIVE DESIGN:
- Frontend only needs to specify: which POI to replace (original_poi) and what to replace it with (replacement_poi)
- Backend handles all the logic: finding the POI, detecting which day it's on, validating the replacement
- Day parameter is optional - backend auto-detects if not provided, and validates/corrects if provided incorrectly
- All backup POIs are preserved - no data loss even if frontend makes mistakes
- Replacement POI must be from the original POI's backup list (validated by backend)

Example Request:
    {
      "replacements": [
        {"original_poi": "Colosseum", "replacement_poi": "Baths of Caracalla"}
      ],
      "mode": "simple",
      "language": "zh-tw"
    }

Backend will:
1. Find "Colosseum" in the tour (any day)
2. Verify "Baths of Caracalla" is in Colosseum's backup list
3. Swap them safely, preserving all other backup options
4. Keep all backup POIs intact for future replacements

Supports two modes:
- simple: Replace POIs and keep current order/timing
- reoptimize: Replace POIs and re-run itinerary optimizer

Args:
    tour_id: Tour identifier
    request: Batch POI replacement request with mode, list of replacements, and language

Returns:
    Batch POI replacement response with success status and new version info

Mark a POI as visited or unvisited.

Args:
    tour_id: Tour identifier
    request: Mark visited request with POI name and status
    language: ISO 639-1 language code (e.g., 'en', 'zh-tw')

Returns:
    Mark visited response with success status

delete.description:
Reset all visit status (deletes visit_status file).

Args:
    tour_id: Tour identifier
    language: ISO 639-1 language code (e.g., 'en', 'zh-tw')

Returns:
    Success response

post.description:
Mark multiple POIs as visited/unvisited in bulk.

Use case: Mark all POIs on a day as visited.

Args:
    tour_id: Tour identifier
    request: Bulk mark visited request with list of POI names
    language: ISO 639-1 language code (e.g., 'en', 'zh-tw')

Returns:
    Bulk mark visited response with success status

get.description:
Get audio generation status for a tour.

Returns the current status of background audio generation including:
- Status (not_started, pending, generating, completed, failed, error)
- Progress percentage
- Number of POIs completed
- Error messages if any

**Authentication Required**: Any authenticated user (client or backstage)

**Use Case**:
- Client app polls this endpoint after tour creation to check audio progress
- Backstage can check audio status for any tour
- Shows "Audio ready" notification when status is "completed"

**Example Response**:
```json
{
  "tour_id": "taipei-tour-123",
  "status": "completed",
  "progress": 100,
  "total_pois": 5,
  "completed_pois": 5,
  "failed_pois": [],
  "provider": "edge",
  "language": "zh-tw"
}
```

List all active user sessions (admin only)

Returns information about all users with active sessions,
separated by backstage and client app users.

post.description:
Migrate all existing tours to have proper visibility settings.

This endpoint marks all existing tours as public and adds creator metadata.
Should be run once after deploying the visibility feature.

Admin only.

replacement_poi.description: Backup POI name to use as replacement

day.title: Day
day.description: Day number where POI is located

POIReplacementRequest.description: Request to replace a POI in a tour (deprecated - use BatchPOIReplacementRequest)

POIReplacementResponse.description: Response after POI replacement (deprecated - use BatchPOIReplacementResponse)

ResearchBasicInfo.description: Basic information about a POI from research

ResearchConcept.description: Concept or theme associated with a POI

ResearchData.description: Complete research data for a POI

ResearchEvent.description: Historical event associated with a POI

ResearchLocation.title: ResearchLocation
ResearchLocation.description: Location or sub-location within a POI

ResumeInfo.title: ResumeInfo
ResumeInfo.description: Resume pointer — the last half-played section. Used by the future Resume feature.

day.title: Day
day.description: Day number this POI belongs to

section_index.title: Section Index
section_index.description: 0-based audio section index within the POI

total_seconds.title: Total Seconds
total_seconds.description: Total length of this section in seconds

completed.title: Completed
completed.description: True when this section played to the end

poi_visited.title: Poi Visited
poi_visited.description: Set True the first time the user physically arrives at this POI on the map

SectionProgressUpdate.title: SectionProgressUpdate
SectionProgressUpdate.description:
Sent on: audio pause, app background, section complete.
One call per section per event — frontend must include current position.

tour_title.title: Tour Title
tour_title.description: Display title e.g. 'Rome's Ancient Wonders · 3 Days'

duration_days.title: Duration Days
duration_days.description: Number of days in the tour

total_pois.title: Total Pois
total_pois.description: Total POI count in the tour

TourAudioProgressResponse.title: TourAudioProgressResponse
TourAudioProgressResponse.description: All audio progress records for a tour (fetched once per session on app open)

status.title: Status
status.description: Audio generation status: not_started, pending, generating, completed, failed, error

total_pois.title: Total Pois
total_pois.description: Total number of POIs in tour

completed_pois.title: Completed Pois
completed_pois.description: Number of POIs with audio generated

failed_pois.title: Failed Pois
failed_pois.description: List of POI names that failed audio generation

error_message.title: Error Message
error_message.description: Error message if status is 'failed' or 'error'

provider.title: Provider
provider.description: TTS provider used (edge, openai, google, qwen)

language.title: Language
language.description: Language code for audio (e.g., zh-tw, en)

TourAudioStatus.title: TourAudioStatus
TourAudioStatus.description: Audio generation status for a tour

TourDay.title: TourDay
TourDay.description: Single day in a tour itinerary

total_duration_hours.title: Total Duration Hours
total_duration_hours.description: Total duration across all days in hours

total_walking_km.title: Total Walking Km
total_walking_km.description: Total walking distance across all days in km

tour_id.title: Tour Id
tour_id.description: Existing tour ID for edit mode; omit for new tours

TourFinalizeRequest.title: TourFinalizeRequest
TourFinalizeRequest.description: Request body for POST /tour/manual/finalize and PUT /tour/manual/{tour_id}

TourFinalizeResponse.title: TourFinalizeResponse
TourFinalizeResponse.description: Response after finalizing a manual tour

days.title: Days
days.description: Number of days for the trip

duration_mode.title: Duration Mode
duration_mode.description: Duration mode: 'days' uses pace-based hours (default), 'hours' uses hours_per_day directly

hours_per_day.title: Hours Per Day
hours_per_day.description: Active hours per day (only used when duration_mode='hours'). Overrides pace-based time budget.

generate_audio.title: Generate Audio
generate_audio.description: Whether to start background audio generation after saving

title_display.title: Title Display
title_display.description: Human-readable display title for the tour (e.g., 'Ancient Rome History · 3 Days')

creator_email.title: Creator Email
creator_email.description: Email of the user who created this tour

narrator_avatar_url.title: Narrator Avatar Url
narrator_avatar_url.description: Relative URL to narrator avatar. Currently null.

cover_image_url.title: Cover Image Url
cover_image_url.description: Relative URL to the tour cover image. Null if no image uploaded.

rating.title: Rating
rating.description: Aggregate star rating (0.0–5.0). Null until review system is implemented.

review_count.title: Review Count
review_count.description: Total submitted reviews. Null until review system is implemented.

poi_id.title: Poi Id
poi_id.description: POI identifier in kebab-case (for URL construction)

distance_to_next_km.title: Distance To Next Km
distance_to_next_km.description: Distance to next POI in km

visited.title: Visited
visited.description: Whether POI has been visited (runtime only)

audio_available.title: Audio Available
audio_available.description: Whether audio is available for this POI

cover_image_url.title: Cover Image Url
cover_image_url.description: Relative URL to a cover photo for this stop. Null if no image uploaded.

blurb.title: Blurb
blurb.description: 1–2 sentence user-facing teaser about this stop. Currently null — placeholder for future content generation.

audio_duration_seconds.title: Audio Duration Seconds
audio_duration_seconds.description: Total audio playback duration across all sections in seconds. Distinct from estimated_hours (visit time).

summary_points.title: Summary Points
summary_points.description: 3–5 key learning points for the whole stop (e.g. 'Gladiator battles were regulated sports, not death matches'). Shown as a 'What you'll learn' list.

sections.title: Sections
sections.description: Audio chapters for this stop. Null if no sectioned transcript exists. Includes title, knowledge_point, and audio URL — avoids a separate transcript fetch for chapter playback.

title.title: Title
title.description: Chapter title shown in the UI

knowledge_point.title: Knowledge Point
knowledge_point.description: One-sentence description shown under the chapter title (e.g. 'How Roman engineers built the world's largest amphitheater')

audio_url.title: Audio Url
audio_url.description: Relative URL to the section audio file. Null if audio not yet generated.

TourPOISection.title: TourPOISection
TourPOISection.description: A single audio chapter within a TourPOI

total_pois.title: Total Pois
total_pois.description: Total number of POIs in tour

TourProgressResponse.title: TourProgressResponse
TourProgressResponse.description: Complete tour progress for authenticated user

TourReview.title: TourReview
TourReview.description: A single user review of a tour

total_stops.title: Total Stops
total_stops.description: Total stops in the tour (same as total_pois, for home feed)

title_display.title: Title Display
title_display.description: Human-readable display title for the tour

cover_image_url.title: Cover Image Url
cover_image_url.description: Relative URL to the tour cover image. Null if no image uploaded.

blurb.title: Blurb
blurb.description: 1-2 sentence teaser shown under the title on tour cards

is_personalized.title: Is Personalized
is_personalized.description: Whether tour was AI-tailored for a specific user

creator_email.title: Creator Email
creator_email.description: Email of the user who created this tour

active.title: Active
active.description: Whether tour is active (soft-delete: false = deactivated)

TourSummary.title: TourSummary
TourSummary.description: Summary of a tour for list view

TourTrailResponse.description: Complete GPS trail for authenticated user

points.title: Points
points.description: GPS points to save (max 100 per request)

TrailUploadRequest.description: Request to upload GPS trail points (batch)

points_saved.title: Points Saved
points_saved.description: Number of points saved in this request

total_points.title: Total Points
total_points.description: Total points in trail after save

language.title: Language

available_languages.title: Available Languages
available_languages.description: List of available language codes for this POI

TranscriptData.description: Transcript and summary data for a POI

linked_at.title: Linked At
linked_at.description: ISO timestamp when link was created

TranscriptLink.title: TranscriptLink
TranscriptLink.description: Link from tour to transcript file

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO timestamp when links were created

TranscriptLinks.title: TranscriptLinks
TranscriptLinks.description: Collection of transcript links for a tour

audio_file.title: Audio File
audio_file.description: Audio filename for this section (e.g., audio_section_1_en.mp3)

TravelMode.description: Travel information for a specific mode

indoor_outdoor.title: Indoor Outdoor
indoor_outdoor.description: Whether POI is indoor, outdoor, or unknown

tour_count.title: Tour Count
tour_count.description: Number of public tours in this city

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: URL-safe slug used in page URL

cover_image_url.description: Cover image URL (absent if not set)

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: URL-safe slug used in page URL

cover_image_url.title: Cover Image Url
cover_image_url.description: Cover image URL (absent if not set)

rating.title: Rating

review_count.description: Number of reviews (null until rating system exists)

duration_label.title: Duration Label
duration_label.description: Human-readable duration e.g. '3 hrs' or '2 days'
